CASE ARCHIVE

Selected Works.

An index of completed structural masterworks, ranging from brutalist cliffside estates to minimal commercial sanctuaries.

Bel Air, CA
12,500 SQFT

The Obsidian Residence

A masterclass in shadow and light, carved from the volcanic soul of the earth.

Conceived as a silent guardian of the Bel Air hillside, utilizing porous raw basalt and carbonized cedar to absorb elements and integrate with rugged canyons.

Porous BasaltCharred Cedar (Shou Sugi Ban)Oversized Glazing
EXPLORE CASE STUDY
Malibu, CA
14,000 SQFT

The Zenith Residence

A stunning ultra-modern villa hovering above the tides of the Pacific Ocean.

Suspended on raw concrete cantilevers, blending high-end gallery-staged interiors with responsive marine salt-resistant protective surfaces.

Travertine SlabsStructural ConcreteWater-Clear Facade Glass
EXPLORE CASE STUDY
London, UK
6,800 SQFT

Avenue Heights

A penthouse of floating steel framing and pristine material intersections.

An urban masterpiece focused on geometric alignment, balancing cold structural steel with beautiful organic white marble grains.

Brushed Stainless SteelPristine Arabescato MarbleAero-acoustic Glazing
EXPLORE CASE STUDY
New York, NY
185,000 SQFT

Corporate Monolith

A massive spatial atrium featuring geometric concrete, high timber, and glass grids.

Reimagining institutional environments as breathing, timber-warmed ecosystems centered around a floating custom wood staircase.

Brutalist Cast ConcreteSustainable Nordic SpruceHigh-Performance Double Glazing
EXPLORE CASE STUDY
Santorini, Greece
18,500 SQFT

Cliffs Edge Retreat

A concrete resort slope harmonized with deep ocean horizons and volcanic terrain.

Raw brutalist power engaging Mediterranean cliffs, offering self-cooling subterranean spaces and infinite salt-water reflecting pools.

Exposed Cast-ConcreteVolcanic Ash AggregateInfinity Water-Walls
EXPLORE CASE STUDY
Kyoto, Japan
5,400 SQFT

The Ivory Pavilion

A meditative pavilion crafted in modular timber, travertine, and shoji glass panels.

Designed around a pristine moss courtyard, using sandblasted travertine and cedar beams to create a quiet, reflective sanctuary.

Travertine StoneSustainable Cedar GridsUltra-clear Shoji Lamilite Glass
EXPLORE CASE STUDY

Crafting monolithic structures and luxury environmental landforms that persist through time.
